name: vercel-deployment
description: Expert knowledge for deploying to Vercel with Next.js Use when: vercel, deploy, deployment, hosting, production.
capabilities: Configure environment variables for dev, preview, and production environments
Choose between Edge and Serverless runtimes for API routes
Optimize builds to reduce cold starts and bundle size
Set up preview deployments for testing before production
Configure monitoring with analytics and error tracking
Avoid common pitfalls like exposing secrets in NEXT_PUBLIC_ variables
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill provides expert knowledge for deploying Next.js applications to Vercel. All static scanner findings were evaluated as false positives: the backticks flagged are markdown inline code formatting, not shell execution; the cryptographic and network flags are common technical terms used in their legitimate context (production deployment, edge functions). No security risks identified.
valueStatement: This skill provides expert guidance for deploying Next.js applications to Vercel, covering environment configuration, edge and serverless functions, build optimization, and common deployment pitfalls.
limitations: This skill does not include actual deployment scripts or CLI commands
Does not provide account-specific configuration for Vercel teams
Does not cover deployment to alternative hosting platforms
Requires basic understanding of Next.js App Router

useCases.targetUser: Junior developers new to Vercel deployment
useCases.title: First-time Vercel deployment
useCases.description: New developers learning how to deploy their first Next.js application to production

useCases.targetUser: Mid-level developers with existing Vercel projects
useCases.title: Optimizing existing deployments
useCases.description: Developers looking to improve performance of their Vercel-hosted applications

useCases.title: Troubleshooting deployment issues
useCases.description: Developers debugging cold start issues, environment variable problems, or function timeouts

promptTemplates.title: Basic deployment question
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I deploy my Next.js app to Vercel?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to understand the basic deployment process

promptTemplates.title: Environment configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: What environment variables do I need to set for my Vercel production deployment?
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs to configure secrets for production

promptTemplates.title: Runtime selection
promptTemplates.prompt: When should I use Edge functions vs Serverless functions on Vercel?
promptTemplates.scenario: User is deciding which runtime to use for their API routes

promptTemplates.title: Build optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: How can I optimize my Vercel build to reduce cold start times and bundle size?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to improve deployment performance

bestPractices: Always use separate databases for preview and production environments to prevent data corruption
Use NEXT_PUBLIC_ only for values that should be visible in the browser
Configure proper caching headers to avoid serving stale content after deployments
antiPatterns: Storing API keys or secrets in NEXT_PUBLIC_ environment variables
Using the same database connection string for all environments
Disabling build cache to 'ensure fresh builds' (hurts performance)

faq.question: Do I need to install the Vercel CLI to deploy?
faq.answer: No. You can deploy directly from GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket by importing your repository in the Vercel dashboard. The CLI is optional but useful for local development.

faq.question: What is the difference between Edge and Serverless functions?
faq.answer: Edge functions run on edge networks closer to users for lower latency but have limited Node.js APIs. Serverless functions have full Node.js support but may have colder starts.

faq.question: Can I use environment variables at build time vs runtime?
faq.answer: Environment variables prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_ are embedded at build time. Other variables are available at runtime. This affects when you can change values without rebuilding.

faq.question: How do preview deployments work?
faq.answer: Preview deployments are automatically created for each pull request. They use their own environment and URL, allowing you to test changes before merging to production.

faq.question: What causes cold starts on Vercel?
faq.answer: Cold starts occur when a serverless function hasn't been invoked recently and needs to initialize. Larger functions and complex dependencies increase cold start time.

faq.question: Can I deploy non-Next.js projects to Vercel?
faq.answer: Yes, Vercel supports many frameworks including React, Vue, Svelte, and static sites. However, this skill focuses specifically on Next.js deployment patterns.
